title: Bug: MCP create_task/close_task persistence handling may coerce tasks into persistent
description:
Investigate suspected MCP compatibility bug where task persistence handling is wrong — user reports tasks seem to all become persistent unexpectedly.

Observed suspicion:
- After recent task operations, tasks appear to become persistent in unexpected ways
- Could be similar to the optional-parameter serialization issue seen with update_task
- Might involve create_task / close_task / task JSON persistence fields being coerced incorrectly by the MCP layer

Investigate:
1. Whether MCP tool calls are serializing `persistent` incorrectly (e.g. empty string, false/string coercion)
2. Whether create_task defaults differ between MCP and REST paths
3. Whether close_task response/behavior is confusing regular vs persistent tasks
4. Whether task JSON files are being written for non-persistent tasks unexpectedly

Why:
If MCP compatibility makes regular tasks become persistent, orchestration becomes very confusing and task lifecycle semantics break.
